sutta: on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was staying nearby sally in great wood at peak roofed house now salha the lichavi came to visit the exalted 1 and on coming to him saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated salhad alichavi said this to the exalted 1 there are some recluses and brahman's lord who proclaim a 2-fold crossing of the flood namely the way made by purity of morals and that made by self-mortification what says the exalted 1 about this lord i do indeed say salha that purity of morals is a factor of reclusion those recluses and brahmins who uphold self-modification who make self-mortification essential who remain clinging to self-mortification they are incapable of crossing the flood moreover salha those recluses and brahmins who practice impurity of body speech and mind who live in impurity they are incapable of knowledge and insight of the enlightenment that is unsurpassed i'll just stop here for a moment here you see just now what i was saying about purity of body speech and mind the buddha is saying here if you have impurity of bodies peace and mind you are incapable of knowledge and insight of the enlightenment that is unsurpassed then the buddha said suppose a man salha desirous of crossing a river takes a sharp axe and enters the jungle there he sees a mighty sultry straight up crooked and young not of crooked growth he cuts it down at the root having done so he lobs it at the top and having lopped it at the top clears off branches and twigs and makes it clean having done so he chips it roughly with excess having chipped it with access he does so with knives having done that he smooths it with a scraper after that he smooths it with a rock ball and then brings it down to the river what do you think salha is that man capable of crossing the river not so lord what is the cause of that why lord the sultry long though well worked outside is not cleaned out inside this is to be expected of it the lord will sing the man comes to misfortune and destruction well salha just in the same way those recluses and brahmins who who uphold self mortification who make self mortification essential who cling to self mortification are incapable of crossing the flood moreover salha those recluses and brahmins who practice impurity of body speech and mind who live in impurity they are incapable of knowledge and insight of the enlightenment which is unsurpassed but on the other hand those recluses and brahmins who do not these things but live purely are capable of knowledge and insight of the enlightenment which is unsurpassed i just stopped here for a while the buddha has given an example about a man trying to make a a a boat out of a long but he carves the outside of the tree he doesn't work on the inside of it of the tree that doesn't it doesn't carve the inside of the tree cast on the outside so similarly self mortification is working on the outside of the body torturing the body and that is not the spiritual path not the aryan path but purity of body speech and mind is purity of the 3 karmas and that is purity of intention because karma is intention so purity of intention is beauty of mind so that is connected with the spiritual path then the buddha said but suppose a man salha desirous of crossing a river takes a sharp axe and enters the jungle there he sees a mighty salt tree trunk etc and after chipping it with knives he takes a chisel and cleans out the inside till it is thoroughly hollowed out then he takes a scraper and scrapes it and smooths it with a rock ball when he has done this he makes a boat of it fastens on ores and rudder and finally brings down the boat to the river what do you think salha is that man capable of crossing the river yes he is lord what is the cause of that why lord that sultry long is well worked outside and thoroughly cleaned out inside made into a bone and fitted with oars and rudder rather this is to be expected of it the boat will not sink and the man will reach the shore in safety well salha just in the same way those recluses and brahmins who are not upholders of self-self mortification who live not clinging to self-mortification are capable of crossing the flood and those recluses and brahmins who practice utter purity in body speech and mind who live in utter purity are capable of knowledge and insight of the enlightenment which is unsurpassed stop here for a while so here the buddha is giving the similarity of the man who works on the long carves the inside of the long outer cleans it and makes it into a boat then he can cross to the other bank so in the same way working on the purity of body speech and mind is working on the purity of our karmas and that means purity of our intentions purity of our mind and that is the spiritual path so the buddha continues just as salha a fighting man because he knows many cunning feats of archery is worthy of a raja is a possession of araja is reckoned an asset to araja in 3 ways what are the 3 he is a far shooter a shooter like lightning a piercer of a huge object this a fighting man is a far shooter even so is the aryan disciple possessed of right concentration the aryan disciple salha who possesses right concentration whatsoever object be it past future or present personal or external to self be it gross or cycle be not exalted far or near every object in short that he beholds he looks upon it as it really is with right wisdom thus this is not mine this am i not not is this myself just as salha a fighting man is a shooter like lightning even source the army's disciple possessed a right view the arab disciple salha who possesses right view understands that as it really is this is dukkha this is the arising of dukkha and this is the ending of dukkha this is the practice going to the ending of dukkha a fighting man is a piercer of a huge object even so the iron disciple is possessed of right liberation the iron disciple who possesses right liberation pierces through the huge mass of ignorance that's the end of the sutta
commentary: now this is 1 of the sutas where the buddha uses the comparison of a fighting man who are in disciple and in the first comparison the buddha said that just as a fighting man is a far shooter even so is the iron disciple possessed of right concentration because the aryan disciple who possesses right concentration sees the 5 kandas the 5 aggregates of 5 grass that aggregates namely body feeling perception abolition and consciousness he sees these 5 aggregates as not mine not i not myself in other words he now these 5 these 5 aggregates are 5 things that all beings associate with the cell they take these 5 things to be the cell or to be um to be a possession of the self or so now the the person who possesses right concentration is able to see things as they really are that these 5 aggregates are not cell not mine not i not myself and so on this shows the importance of right concentration uh because only 1 with white right concentration can see things as they really are now right concentration in the buddha's teachings in the suttas is always defined as the 4 janas or 1 pointedness of mind the 1 pointlessness of mine is explained very clearly in suta in the nikaya sutra number 35.206 where the buddha used the simile of 6 animals and these 6 animals when they are tied together the 6 animals are trying to take off in 6 different directions so that at any 1 time whichever 1 is strongest will pull the others and when it becomes tired and then another moment whichever 1 is strongest will pull and the the other 5 will have to follow him so this is um supposed to be the everyday mind because uh our the our 6 senses uh there are 6 objects which impinge on our 6 senses on our sixth sense doors and whichever 1 a strongest world uh pull our attention and then the buddha said that is not to the way to tame the 6 animals the way to tame the 6 animals is to tie all the 6 animals around a huge as a stout pole so that whichever direction they try to take off they can only go round and round that pole so that eventually they become tired and weary and they lie down tamed beside the pole so in the same way to tame the mine we have to tie to 1 stout pawn in other words 1 object and whenever it is pulled away in a in a different direction we have to pull it back to that 1 object of meditation so that eventually the mind becomes tamed and we attain 1 pointedness of mind now putting our attention on different objects from moment to moment is just mindfulness being mindful of the sixth sense doors and mindfulness is the seventh factor of the rna4 path mindfulness is sati and that is a tense contemplation or putting our attention from object to object a different object to a different object whereas a right concentration of perfect concentration is putting our attention on 1 object from moment to moment to moment always on 1 object and and that is the way to get 1 pointedness of mind so um mindfulness and perfect concentration are different factors of the iron eightfold path the seventh and the eighth factor and they should not be confused they are not the same and this is very important that we bear this in mind because some people confuse right concentration with mindfulness they think that concentration is putting our attention from object to or to different object from moment to moment but this is clearly not so as explained in the samyutanikaya 35.206 now the second comparison is just like the fighting man is a shooter like lightning even so as the iron disciple possess the right view because they are impossible the disciple who possesses right view understands as it really is therefore our includes about dukkha you are suffering and so you can see that um the the person with right view who understands the phone are in truth is an area so um right view is equivalent to being an arya and according to angutta nikaya 2.11.9 [Music] there are only 2 conditions for attaining right view namely uh listening to the teachings and having proper attention so from here you can see that the way to attain right view and hence a stream entry is by listening to the sutas with proper attention and there's another suta which confirms this name list on utah nikaya 46.
